hero-lede: A good infographic is a big, clear picture — bold sections, little icons, a few honest numbers. You can take the whole thing in at one look. Ours have one extra thing: each has a live bit you can poke. First read it like a poster on a wall. Then change something, and watch the picture answer you back.

All the at-a-glance clarity of a good infographic — bold sections, icons, a few honest numbers — plus one extra thing: each has a live bit you can poke. Read it like a poster first, then change something and watch it respond.

The at-a-glance clarity of a good infographic — bold sections, icons, a few honest numbers — with one difference: each has a live bit you can poke. Read it like a poster, then change something and watch it respond.

A good infographic’s at-a-glance clarity — bold sections, icons, a few honest numbers — with one difference: each carries a live bit you can poke. Read it as a poster, then change something and watch it respond.
Infographic clarity — bold sections, icons, honest numbers — plus a live bit you can poke. Read as a poster; change something; watch it respond.
ig-vs: These aren’t the same as an explorable. An explorable is a whole working model that you drive yourself, end to end. An infographic here is mostly a picture someone has designed for you — with one part you can play with. First you’re shown; then you have a poke.
